articles.title: Donald Trump Doesn’t Understand The Adage About Catching Flies With Honey — See Also
articles.content:
**证据开示确实是诉讼中最精彩的部分：** 美国律师协会要求白宫公开那些针对大型律所的行政命令细节。

**全球最佳法学院：** 美国院校在此排名中占据显著位置。

**CSI智能体：** Exterro公司发布用于取证调查的智能体人工智能工具，其快速、可审计的特性或将引发法庭对其证据可采性的激烈辩论。

**注定失败：** 唐纳德·特朗普在向最高法院提出重大请求前，竟称其"完全疯狂"。

原文链接：[唐纳德·特朗普不懂“以蜜捕蝇”的谚语——另见] 首发于法律新闻网站Above the Law。
articles.summary:
Discovery Really Is The Best Part Of Litigation: ABA wants the deets on the Trump Biglaw executive orders.

The World’s Best Law Schools: Of course the USA features prominently on this ranking.

CSI Agentic: Exterro releases an agentic AI tool for forensics. Courts will take a while to come to grips with this.

Born To Lose: Donald Trump asks Supreme Court for big favor by calling them insane.

articles.title: On The Other Side Of The 4th
articles.content:
这个假期周末的天气不讲情面。"残酷"都算轻描淡写。气候变化是真实的，如果你不信我，那就去读读杰夫·古德尔几年前出版的书《高温会先杀死你：灼热星球上的生与死》。我不是来辩论气候变化问题的。你只需要环顾四周，如果你不这样做，那是你的耻辱。我们可以把不切实际的想法留给47和他的同伙；他控制不了天气，至少目前还不行。也去读读古德尔关于海平面上升的书。我不知道哪本书更可怕。

还记得那个让47起家的电视节目《学徒》吗？还记得他那句夸大其词的话吗？"你被解雇了！"鉴于最高法院现在认可了他解雇政府高级官员的权利，47一定在沾沾自喜。在6比3的裁决中，他们对独立机构真正独立因此可随心所欲终止的说法嗤之以鼻。（我想知道当民主党人可以随心所欲地雇用和解雇时，公众会有什么反应。只是问问。）然而，总统权力只有一个例外，那就是不能解雇美联储成员。莉萨·库克仍然担任美联储成员，尽管针对她伪造贷款文件的诉讼仍在进行中。

为最高法院罕见地给予邮寄选票的胜利欢呼。作为加州居民，我所在的18个允许邮寄投票的州之一，我感到欣慰。有了邮寄选票，完全没有理由不去投票。投票至关重要，无论你选择投票给谁。是的，我有自己的偏好，但投票权是许多其他国家没有（也可能永远不会）拥有的权利。250周年纪念不就是为了这个吗？有了邮寄选票，说你没时间投票是个站不住脚的借口，特别是在那些允许工作时间投票的州，加州就是其中之一。

经过50年的执业律师生涯，我仍然对律师在法庭上的轻率行为感到震惊。例证之一是司法部和联邦地区法官莱昂妮·布林克玛之间持续的对峙。法院拒绝接受司法部关于已摧毁47想要的武器化基金的口头陈述，并命令在伪证罪处罚下做出声明。司法部律师告诉法院"法院的要求是不必要的"，也就是"想得美"。

哇，如果我或几乎任何其他律师这样回应，我们会被法警立即带走，有时间重新考虑如何对法庭说话。布林克玛法官裁定针对47一月专项资金的诉讼并非撤销，并安排了辩论。这里有任何伦理问题吗？讨论一下。

在洛杉矶一个备受关注的案件中，陪审团以10比2支持无罪开释。还记得2025年1月初摧毁性的帕萨迪纳大火吗？（更不用说摧毁了圣加布里埃尔山麓社区的伊顿大火了。）帕萨迪纳火灾的一个有争议的问题是火灾是如何开始的。1月1日发生了一场小火，洛杉矶消防局将其扑灭。但完全扑灭了吗？被司法部起诉的帕萨迪纳火灾的年轻被告不能逍遥法外，他仍在狱中，等待重审。在陪审团以大多数票支持无罪开释的情况下，重审有意义吗？

法院确实用艾伦指令（有时被称为"炸药指令"）指示了陪审团，基本上是说没有其他陪审团能更好地达成裁决，并命令他们继续审议。但这没有奏效。

洛杉矶的美国助理检察官希望在重审中拥有"更好的陪审员"。祝他好运。只是因为10名陪审员不相信控方的案件理论，这是否就使他们成为不合格的陪审员？我不这么认为。控方仍然必须排除合理怀疑地证明其案件。这里有任何伦理问题吗？讨论一下。

这么多人失去了一切，包括生命。说气候变化没有起到任何作用是荒谬的。对下一次政府将拥有"更好的陪审员"的说法表示反对。鉴于司法部是47的走狗，这有什么奇怪的吗？讨论一下。

吉尔·斯威策是加州律师协会的活跃成员，已超过40年。她记得法律行业曾经更友善、更温和的时光。她的法律职业生涯多元，包括担任副地方检察官、独立执业和几家高级内部职位。她现在全职从事调解工作，这让她有机会看到恐龙一代、千禧一代和介于两者之间的人互动——并不总是文明的。你可以通过电子邮件oldladylawyer@gmail.com联系她。

本文《On The Other Side Of The 4th》首次发布于Above the Law。
articles.summary:
Weather didn’t discriminate this past holiday weekend. Brutal is putting it mildly. Climate change is real and if you don’t believe me, then read Jeff Goodell’s book of several years ago, “The Heat Will Kill You First: Life and Death on a Scorched Planet.”

I am not here to debate climate change issues. All you have to do is look around you, and if you don’t, shame on you. We can leave unreality to 47 and his peeps; he can’t control the weather, at least not yet. And also read Goodell’s book on rising seas. I don’t know which book is scarier.

Remember the TV show “The Apprentice” that gave 47 his start? Remember his bombastic sentence? “You’re fired!” 47 must be rubbing his little hands with glee given that the Supreme Court has now validated his right to fire top government officials. In a 6-3 decision, they pooh-poohed the idea that independent agencies are truly independent and thus are subject to termination by whim. (I wonder what the outcry will be when Democrats are free to hire and fire at will. Just asking.) However, the only exception to a president’s free will is that they cannot fire a member of the Federal Reserve. Lisa Cook still has her job as a Fed member while the lawsuit claiming she falsified loan documents wends its way.

And yay for the rare victory that the Supreme Court gave to mail-in ballots. As a California resident of one of the 18 states that allows mail-in ballots, I am delighted. With mail-in ballots, there is absolutely no excuse for not voting. Voting is critical, regardless of who you choose to vote for. Yes, I have my preferences, but the right to vote is something that many other countries do not (and may never) have. Isn’t that what the 250th anniversary was all about? With mail-in ballots, saying that you don’t or didn’t have time to vote is a flimsy excuse, especially in those states that allow time off from work to vote, California being one of them.

Even after 50 years as a practicing lawyer, I am still gobsmacked at how cavalierly counsel can behave in court. Exhibit 1 is the continuing runaround between the DOJ and federal district judge Leonie Brinkema. The court refused to accept the DOJ’s oral representation that it had driven a stake through the heart of the proposed weaponization fund that 47 wanted. The court ordered declarations to that effect be made under penalty of perjury. The DOJ lawyer told the court that “the court’s demands were unnecessary,” aka “pound sand.” 

Whoa, if I or just about any other lawyer had so responded, we would have been in the custody of the sheriff forthwith, with time to reconsider how to speak to the court. Judge Brinkema determined that the lawsuit challenging 47’s January slush fund was not moot and scheduled briefing. Any ethical issue here? Discuss.

In a closely watched case here in Los Angeles, 10-2 for acquittal was the result. Remember the devastating Palisades fire of early January  2025? (Not to mention the Eaton fire that devastated communities in the San Gabriel foothills.) One controversial issue in the Palisades fire has been how that fire started. A small fire started on January 1, and LAFD extinguished it. But completely? The young man prosecuted by the DOJ for the Palisades fire doesn’t get to walk away, he’s still in jail, awaiting retrial. With a hung jury that favored acquittal by a large margin, does retrial make sense?

The court did instruct the jury with the Allen charge, sometimes referred to as the “dynamite charge,” which essentially says that no other jury could do a better job of reaching a verdict and ordered them to continue deliberations. But it didn’t work. 

The First Assistant US Attorney here in Los Angeles hopes that in the retrial, he will have “better jurors.” Good luck with that. Just because 10 of the jurors didn’t buy the prosecution’s theory of the case does that ipso facto make them bad jurors? I don’t think so. The prosecution still must prove its case beyond a reasonable doubt. Any ethical issue here? Discuss.

So many people lost everything, including lives. It’s ridiculous to say that climate change played no part. Phooey on the claim that next time around the government will have “better jurors.” Given that the DOJ is 47’s lap dog, is that any surprise? Discuss.

Jill Switzer has been an active member of the State Bar of California for over 40 years. She remembers practicing law in a kinder, gentler time. She’s had a diverse legal career, including stints as a deputy district attorney, a solo practice, and several senior in-house gigs. She now mediates full-time, which gives her the opportunity to see dinosaurs, millennials, and those in-between interact — it’s not always civil. You can reach her by email at oldladylawyer@gmail.com.

The post On The Other Side Of The 4th appeared first on Above the Law.

articles.title: Trump Calls Supreme Court ‘Absolutely Insane’ And Then Asks For Birthright Citizenship Do-Over
articles.content:
特朗普在"特朗普诉芭芭拉案"中败诉，是本任期最不出人意料的判决。但这也是最令人意外的判决之一——尽管第十四修正案措辞明确，一年前几乎无人质疑其含义，几位大法官却决定采纳"伊莱·卡什主义"，宣称"也许并非如此？"托马斯大法官那90多页排外主义幻想小说严重损害了法院尊严，但最终对判决毫无影响。出生公民权自第十四修正案批准以来就是法律，不能被行政命令一笔勾销。

特朗普曾说："我们会赢得太多，你们可能会厌倦胜利。"显然他已经厌倦了，想再让最高法院给他一个"L"。昨天打盹间隙，他在"真实社交"上宣布将要求同一法院"立即"重新审理整个案件，因为判决"绝对疯狂"且是"司法不公"。

用"绝对疯狂"作为申请复审的开场白有用吗？问这话的人像个白痴。

边境线上没有广告牌宣传4000美元"送货"服务——美国99.9%的广告牌空间都被摩根 & 摩根占据了。特朗普似乎从德克萨斯州官员推动的虚假愤怒中提取了这个说法，声称2021年一家提供低价产科服务的地区医院相当于引诱移民穿越边境。

和这家伙相关的一切都像传话游戏，只是第一个说话的人是个白人至上主义者，最后一个是个患有痴呆症的白人至上主义者。

无论如何，特朗普不会获得复审机会。史蒂夫·弗拉代克几年前分析过最高法院实践中这个技术性角落，当时自由派还在幻想特朗普豁免权案能够复审。他关于第44条规则的结论仍然成立。法院上一次在辩论案件中批准复审是在1965年，上一次在复审中推翻判决是在1956年——里德诉科弗特案是法院整个历史上唯一一次这样的逆转。

投票数学比程序障碍更不利。根据第44条规则，实体复审不能被批准，除非法院多数通过，且只能由在判决中投赞成票的大法官发起。三位完全反对的大法官——托马斯、阿利托和戈萨奇——不能发起复审投票。卡瓦诺加入了判决但不同意多数意见的宪法结论，他仍需说服罗伯茨或巴雷特在几周内基于什么都没有的基础上彻底改变主意。

坦白说，如果他们中的任何一个真的这样做了，应该立即引发关于敲诈的质疑。尽管也许威胁最高法院大法官在"特朗普诉美国案"中属于"官方行为"。

司法部长应该明白这是一份毫无意义的动议。但他愿意花一个多小时用"罗马法渊源"的暗示来挑战宪法基本原则，所以他已经证明为了留在这个政府里他愿意接受任何程度的职业羞辱。一份轻率的动议是通过忠诚测试的小代价。

这就是忠诚体系的全部架构——要求永不解决，只会升级，而留下的代价就是按命令做下一件绝望的事。特朗普已经告诉他任命的大法官们，他期望他们的投票是一笔个人债务。现在他期望他的司法部长能够"反败为胜"。这些东西没有一件有效，但他们会继续嘲弄这个体制，以取悦他们任性的老板。

你让这家伙推翻一张红牌，突然他就认为他可以对任何事情这样做。就好像他认为最高法院和FIFA一样不择手段。但与大法官们的自尊不允许他们承认自己可能错了。如果他真的获得复审机会，结果大概会和那张红牌一样糟糕。

这篇文章首次发表于Above the Law。
articles.summary:
Donald Trump losing Trump v. Barbara was the least surprising ruling of the term. That said, it was one of the more surprising decisions because while the Fourteenth Amendment is clear and literally no one doubted its meaning until about a year ago, a few of the justices decided that to embrace the “Eli Cash Doctrine” and declare “maybe it isn’t?” The intellectual dishonesty on display, especially in Clarence Thomas’s 90+ page collection of nativist fan fiction, demeaned the Court, but was ultimately inconsequential to the outcome. Birthright citizenship is, as it has been since the ratification of the Fourteenth Amendment, the law of the land. It cannot be waved away by executive order.

Trump once said, “We’re going to win so much, you may even get tired of winning.” Apparently, he has. Because he wants to be dealt another Supreme Court L.

In between naps yesterday, Trump announced on Truth Social that he will ask the same Court to do the whole thing over again — “IMMEDIATELY” — because the decision was “absolutely insane” and a “miscarriage of justice.”

Does it help a motion for rehearing to preface it with “absolutely insane”? Asking for an idiot.

There are no signs and billboards all along the border advertising $4000 deliveries. Where would they even put them? By my rough estimation, 99.9 percent of the nation’s billboard space is taken up by Morgan & Morgan. Trump seems to draw this $4000 claim from a different faux outrage pushed by Texas officials, claiming that a regional hospital offering low flat fee obstetrician services in 2021 amounted to enticing migrants to cross the border.

Everything with this guy is like a game of telephone, except the first player is a white nationalist and the last is a white nationalist with dementia.

Anyway, Trump will not get a rehearing. Steve Vladeck walked through this nerdy corner of Supreme Court practice a couple years ago when liberals were fantasizing about a rehearing in the Trump immunity case. His conclusions about Rule 44 still hold. The Court last granted rehearing in an argued case in 1965, and even that was only to tweak the judgment. The last time it reheard a case and actually reversed its decision on rehearing was in 1956. That case, Reid v. Covert, marks the only such reversal in the Court’s entire history.

And the vote math is worse than the procedural hurdle. Under Rule 44, a merits rehearing cannot be granted except by a majority of the Court, and only at the instance of a justice who concurred in the judgment. The three full dissenters — Thomas, Alito, and Gorsuch — cannot initiate a rehearing vote. Technically, Kavanaugh joined the judgment but disagreed with the majority’s constitutional conclusion, so maybe that’s enough to get the ball rolling, but he’d still need to convince Roberts or Barrett to radically change their minds within a matter of weeks based on… nothing.

Frankly, under those facts, if one of them did, it should trigger an immediate question about extortion. Though maybe threatening Supreme Court justices is an “official act” under Trump v. United States.

That would require shame. The Solicitor General, presumably, understands that this is a nonsense motion. But he was willing to spend over an hour challenging a bedrock principle of constitutional law with allusions to “Roman law sources” so he’s proven he’s up for any degree of professional debasement to stay in this administration. He just finished asking the Court to reconsider its refusal to hear his E. Jean Carroll appeal, so baseless rehearing petitions are fast becoming a reflex. A frivolous motion is a small price to pay to pass a loyalty test.

Unless we’re going to get serious as a profession about penalizing this conduct.

That is the whole architecture of a loyalty regime — the demand never resolves, it escalates, and the cost of staying is doing the next hopeless thing on command. Trump already told the justices he appointed that he expects their votes as a personal debt. Now he expects his Solicitor General to un-lose a case. None of this stuff works, but they’ll keep making a mockery of the system to keep on the happy side of their petulant boss.

You let this guy overturn a red card and suddenly he thinks you can do that with everything. It’s as though he thinks the Supreme Court is as unprincipled as FIFA. Which it is, but unlike FIFA, the justices’ egos prevent admitting that they could be wrong. And if he were to secure a rehearing, it would end just about as well as that red card did.

Earlier: Supreme Court Narrowly Passes Reading Comprehension Section
Justice Gorsuch’s Birthright Citizenship Dissent… Will Not Make Donald Trump Happy
Donald Trump Went To Supreme Court To Watch Live As Birthright Citizenship Policy Got Thoroughly Smoked

Joe Patrice is a senior editor at Above the Law and co-host of Thinking Like A Lawyer. Feel free to email any tips, questions, or comments. Follow him on Twitter or Bluesky if you’re interested in law, politics, and a healthy dose of college sports news.

The post Trump Calls Supreme Court ‘Absolutely Insane’ And Then Asks For Birthright Citizenship Do-Over appeared first on Above the Law.
